'''Tom Whitehead''' is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]], on loan from [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].

==Playing career==
===Warrington===
In 2022 Whitehead made his début for the [[Warrington Wolves|Wire]] in the Super League against the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62752706 Super League: Salford Red Devils 14-32 Warrington - Wolves beat under-strength hosts]</ref>

===Rochdale Hornets (loan)===
He has spent time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]].

===Widnes (DR)===
On 3 January 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on DR-loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=3 Jan 2024|title=Warrington Wolves duo link up with Championship neighbours via dual-registration: ‘They want to prove they can go all the way’|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-wolves-duo-link-up-with-championship-neighbours-via-dual-registration-they-want-to-prove-they-can-go-all-the-way}}</ref>

===London Broncos (DR)===
On 28 March 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on [[RFL dual registration|DR]] loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=28 Mar 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Warrington Wolves’ new dual-registration partners announced as Championship club confirm duo’s arrival|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-wolves-new-dual-registration-partners-announced-as-championship-club-confirm-duos-arrival}}</ref>

===Halifax Panthers (loan)===
On 16 April 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=16 Apr 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Warrington Wolves young gun completes switch to Championship frontrunners|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-wolves-young-gun-completes-switch-to-championship-frontrunners}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils (loan)===
On 17 June 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]] on rolling-monthly loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=17 Jun 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Warrington duo join Salford Red Devils as Sam Stone swap complete|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-duo-join-salford-red-devils-as-sam-stone-swap-complete}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC (loan)===
On 14 August 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan for the remainder of the 2025 season<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=14 Aug 2025|author=George Riley|title=Salford Red Devils’ latest blow confirmed as Warrington Wolves send loanee to Championship|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-red-devils-latest-blow-confirmed-as-warrington-wolves-send-loanee-to-championship}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 17 November 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] on a 3-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=17 Nov 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Hull KR swoop to sign Warrington Wolves starlet ahead of 2026 as long-term deal announced|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-sign-tom-whitehead-ahead-of-2026}}</ref>

===Doncaster RLFC (loan)===
On 4 June 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=4 Jun 2026|author=Doug Thomson|title=Hull KR forward ready to dig in for Doncaster|url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-kr-forward-ready-to-dig-in-for-doncaster/}}</ref>
